goal: Land the Graphiti (G3 Knowledge, P1 priority) external MCP plugin manifest with a real-subprocess `tools/list` health-check integration test as the THIRD test inside the existing `mod g3_plugin_manifests { ... }` block in tests/g3_plugin_manifests.rs, mirroring the WO-0069 codebase-memory + mem0 template and following the Serena (WO-0013) `uvx --from git+ref` precedent. Reverses DEC-0019's premature deferral per DEC-0022 (the canonical graphiti MCP server is shipped as a getzep/graphiti `mcp_server/` subdirectory installable via `uvx --from git+...` rather than a standalone PyPI/npm package).
plan_summary: Solo-feature G3 plugin-manifest landing WO mirroring WO-0072 (codegraphcontext G4 solo-feature shape) extended with WO-0076 (operator-state-CLI-dependent + graceful skip) lessons. Add plugins/knowledge/graphiti/plugin.toml + scripts/devtools/install-graphiti-mcp.sh + replacement scripts/verify/P3-W9-F10.sh + ONE new test `graphiti_manifest_health_check` inserted into the existing `mod g3_plugin_manifests { ... }` block in crates/ucil-daemon/tests/g3_plugin_manifests.rs (between the existing `mem0_manifest_health_check` and the module's closing brace, additive only — DEC-0005 module-coherence carve-out for the small ~60-LOC commit). Install path: `uvx --from git+https://github.com/getzep/graphiti.git@<commit-sha>#subdirectory=mcp_server graphiti_mcp_server` (Serena precedent at plugins/structural/serena/plugin.toml — uvx --from git+ ref with a SHA-pinned commit honors AC15 pinned-immutable-tag policy). Docker-image (`zepai/knowledge-graph-mcp`) is the SECONDARY path documented in scope_in but discouraged unless uvx/git fails (UCIL has no existing docker-installed plugins; adding one would be net-new transport infrastructure). Operator-state short-circuits per WO-0076 §executor lesson: graphiti requires (a) a graph DB connection (FalkorDB or Neo4j) and (b) an LLM API key (OPENAI_API_KEY default) — both gated in the verify script's tool-level smoke section with `[SKIP] P3-W9-F10: <reason>` + `exit 0` if either is unset; the cargo-test `tools/list` path remains the load-bearing assertion. Pre-baked M1/M2 mutation contract per the 6-WO mature pattern (WO-0066/0067/0068/0069/0070/0072/0077). F10 has `attempts=2`; this WO is the third+final attempt — clean execution required to avoid the 3-strikes soft escalation trigger.
scope_in: 1. PRIMARY install path: `uvx --from git+https://github.com/getzep/graphiti.git@<COMMIT-SHA>#subdirectory=mcp_server graphiti_mcp_server` — mirroring the Serena precedent at plugins/structural/serena/plugin.toml lines 21-32. The COMMIT-SHA must be a 40-character hex SHA captured at WO-execution time from `gh api repos/getzep/graphiti/commits/main --jq '.sha'` (or equivalent), pinning to the upstream's then-current tip. Tags MAY be used INSTEAD of SHAs only if the tag is not a moving ref (e.g., `@v0.x.y` is acceptable IF v0.x.y is a frozen release tag, NOT a moving branch). The fallback `git+...@main` floating-ref is FORBIDDEN per AC15 + master-plan §13.

2. SECONDARY install path (fallback only — document in scope_in but DO NOT use unless PRIMARY fails): `docker run -i --rm --network <ucil-graphiti-net> zepai/knowledge-graph-mcp:<TAG>` per DEC-0022 §Decision point 2. UCIL currently has zero docker-installed plugins; adding one would be net-new infrastructure (operator must `docker pull` + start FalkorDB/Neo4j sidecar + create docker network). If the executor's PRIMARY-path live capture (uvx --from git+ ref) succeeds in producing a clean `tools/list` MCP handshake, the docker path is NOT needed and should NOT be wired. Document this decision tree in the manifest's leading comment block.

3. Create plugins/knowledge/graphiti/plugin.toml mirroring the structure of plugins/knowledge/mem0/plugin.toml (P3-W9-F06 / WO-0069): leading multi-line comment with `pinned <DATE>:` block (source URL, commit SHA, install command, advertised tools list with count, transport=stdio, operator-state requirements section listing the graph DB env vars [FALKORDB_URI or NEO4J_URI per upstream README] and LLM API key env vars [OPENAI_API_KEY default; ANTHROPIC_API_KEY / GROQ_API_KEY / etc. per upstream's LLM-provider-pluggability]); `[plugin]` section (name='graphiti', version=<pinned commit SHA short form e.g. '0.x.y-SHA12345' or full SHA per upstream tag scheme>, description citing master-plan §4.3 line 313 + §5.3 G3 source designation + 'bi-temporal knowledge graph with validity windows; hallmark of the Graphiti P1 plugin' per master-plan §17.2); `[capabilities]` section with provides=['knowledge.temporal_query', 'knowledge.fact_validity_lookup'] (or analogous knowledge.* names — namespace is established by codebase-memory + mem0 in WO-0069, these new capabilities EXTEND it cleanly per master-plan §5.3 'temporal merge'); languages=[] (the temporal knowledge graph is text-keyed agent observations, NOT code-language-keyed, mirroring mem0); `[transport]` section with type='stdio', command='uvx', args=['--from', 'git+https://github.com/getzep/graphiti.git@<COMMIT-SHA>#subdirectory=mcp_server', 'graphiti_mcp_server'] (executor verifies the upstream `mcp_server/` subdirectory's actual entry-point name from its `pyproject.toml` console_scripts table); `[lifecycle]` section mirroring F05/F06 exactly (hot_cold=true, idle_timeout_minutes=10).

4. The `transport.command` field MUST be the bare binary name `"uvx"` (NOT an absolute path) — per WO-0069 lessons §executor: the M1 transport.command-poison mutation pattern relies on swapping to `/__ucil_test_nonexistent_M1__`, AND absolute paths break across CI/dev environments.

5. Capture the live `tools/list` AT THE SPECIFIC PINNED COMMIT-SHA before sealing the manifest's `[capabilities] provides` list and the test's expected-tool-name literal — per WO-0076 §planner lesson 2. Steps: (a) install via the chosen pinned ref (`uvx --from git+...@<SHA>#subdirectory=mcp_server graphiti_mcp_server --help` or equivalent), (b) drive the MCP `initialize` → `notifications/initialized` → `tools/list` handshake via a one-shot Python driver script (mirror the WO-0067/0069/0075/0076 `/tmp/wo-NNNN-capture.py` shape), (c) capture the full advertised tool-list verbatim and embed it in the manifest's top-of-file rustdoc + the test's SA-tagged panic literal. NB: If graphiti's MCP server requires graph-DB connectivity to RESPOND to `initialize` (i.e. blocks tools/list until FalkorDB/Neo4j is reachable), the executor MUST: (i) document this fact prominently in the manifest's rustdoc operator-state-requirements block, (ii) gate the integration test's actual `health_check_with_timeout` call on `UCIL_SKIP_KNOWLEDGE_PLUGIN_E2E=0 AND <GRAPH_DB_ENV>=set` (extending the WO-0069 MEM0_API_KEY-style two-gate pattern), and (iii) keep the `tools/list` cargo-test path load-bearing only when the graph DB env is set — when unset, the test honours the skip-via-env early-return per WO-0069 line 34 precedent.

6. Operator-state-CLI-dependent classification per WO-0077 §planner lesson 3: graphiti is operator-state-DUAL-DEPENDENT — it requires BOTH a graph DB connection AND an LLM API key. Document in scope_in's lifespan-handler section (manifest top-of-file rustdoc) and reflect in the verify script's two-gate short-circuit pattern. The two-gate pattern: `if [[ "${UCIL_SKIP_KNOWLEDGE_PLUGIN_E2E:-0}" != "0" ]] || [[ -z "${FALKORDB_URI:-}${NEO4J_URI:-}" ]] || [[ -z "${OPENAI_API_KEY:-}" ]]; then echo '[SKIP] P3-W9-F10: tool-level smoke skipped — graph DB or OPENAI_API_KEY not set'; exit 0; fi` — the cargo-test `tools/list` path (Step 4 of the verify script) remains the load-bearing assertion regardless. (Adapt to whichever LLM-provider env-var graphiti's MCP server's lifespan handler requires per the executor's live capture.)

7. Extend crates/ucil-daemon/tests/g3_plugin_manifests.rs by ADDING a third test `graphiti_manifest_health_check` INSIDE the existing `mod g3_plugin_manifests { ... }` block (between the closing brace of the existing `mem0_manifest_health_check` and the module's closing `}`). Mirror the existing two tests' shape exactly: `#[tokio::test] async fn`, `if skip_via_env() { return; }`, load manifest, manifest sanity asserts (name='graphiti', transport.kind='stdio', !provides.is_empty()), `PluginManager::health_check_with_timeout(&manifest, FIRST_RUN_TIMEOUT_MS)`, assert health.name='graphiti', health.status=Ok, !health.tools.is_empty(), and a load-bearing assertion that one specific advertised tool name is present (executor selects the canonical Graphiti MCP tool from the live capture — likely `add_episode` / `search_facts` / `add_observation` / `search_memory_nodes` per the upstream `mcp_server/graphiti_mcp_server.py` source — and pins on it; rationale comment cites that pinning on the count would force updates on every benign upstream tool addition while pinning on a specific name surfaces upstream tool-surface drift loudly, mirroring the mem0 `add_memory` / codebase-memory `search_graph` rationale).

8. ADDITIVELY only — do NOT modify the existing two tests inside `mod g3_plugin_manifests { ... }` (codebase_memory_manifest_health_check, mem0_manifest_health_check). Confirm via `git diff main -- crates/ucil-daemon/tests/g3_plugin_manifests.rs | grep -E '^-' | grep -vE '^---' | wc -l` returning 0 (zero deletions; only added lines). Per WO-0069 lessons §executor: the existing module's two-test scope must not be mutated by additions.

9. The new test lives INSIDE the existing `mod g3_plugin_manifests { ... }` block at module root (DEC-0007 frozen-selector module-root placement, carried per WO-0068 lessons §planner 'frozen-test selector substring-match REQUIRES module-root placement'). Confirm `cargo test -p ucil-daemon --test g3_plugin_manifests g3_plugin_manifests::graphiti_manifest_health_check` resolves uniquely (substring-matches without requiring `--exact`).

10. Create scripts/devtools/install-graphiti-mcp.sh mirroring scripts/devtools/install-mem0-mcp.sh verbatim in shape: `set -euo pipefail`, `PINNED_GRAPHITI_COMMIT_SHA=` constant, OK-when-uvx-on-PATH branch, MISSING branch with the exact pinned install command (`uvx --from git+https://github.com/getzep/graphiti.git@<SHA>#subdirectory=mcp_server graphiti_mcp_server --help` for the warm-up; `--help` may not be honored by graphiti's MCP server — that's fine, the uvx fetch primes the cache as a side-effect anyway, mirroring WO-0075 lesson §executor for filesystem-mcp). Always `exit 0` (informational scaffolding — same contract as WO-0069/0072/0075/0076/0077).

11. Replace scripts/verify/P3-W9-F10.sh (currently TODO-stub) by copying scripts/verify/P3-W9-F06.sh verbatim and adapting: change pinned-version constant to the graphiti commit-sha, change the cargo-test selector to `cargo test -p ucil-daemon --test g3_plugin_manifests g3_plugin_manifests::graphiti_manifest_health_check`, and adapt the tool-level smoke section per scope_in #6 (two-gate pattern: UCIL_SKIP_KNOWLEDGE_PLUGIN_E2E AND graph-DB-env AND LLM-API-key-env all required for the smoke to fire). The smoke MUST exercise at least ONE end-to-end tool-call against an ephemeral graph (e.g., `tools/call add_episode` then `tools/call search_facts`) when all gates pass; when any gate misses, `[SKIP]`-exit 0 with a clear diagnostic. Print `[OK] P3-W9-F10` on green; `[FAIL] P3-W9-F10: <reason>` on red. Always `exit 0` on green / `exit 1` on red. The script remains read-only against tests/fixtures/** (forbidden_paths invariant).

12. Pre-baked mutation contract M1 (graphiti transport.command poison): swap `command = "uvx"` → `command = "/__ucil_test_nonexistent_M1__"` in plugins/knowledge/graphiti/plugin.toml; cargo test selector `cargo test -p ucil-daemon --test g3_plugin_manifests g3_plugin_manifests::graphiti_manifest_health_check` must FAIL with `PluginError::Spawn { source: Os { code: 2, kind: NotFound } }` (or the analogous spawn-error chain on the platform); restore via `git checkout -- plugins/knowledge/graphiti/plugin.toml`; confirm md5sum matches the pre-mutation snapshot.

13. Pre-baked mutation contract M2 (expected-tool-name regression): in tests/g3_plugin_manifests.rs's new `graphiti_manifest_health_check` test, change the `t == "<canonical_graphiti_tool>"` assertion to a non-existent tool name (e.g., `t == "NON_EXISTENT_TOOL_M2"`); the test MUST FAIL with the structured panic carrying the full advertised-tool list (the `(SAn) <semantic name>; left: ..., right: ...` panic body itself doubles as a diagnostic dump of the live tool surface at the pinned commit-sha). Restore via `git checkout -- crates/ucil-daemon/tests/g3_plugin_manifests.rs`; confirm md5sum match.

14. Pre-flight word-ban grep MUST cover ONLY production-side files (manifest, install script, verify script) and EXEMPT integration-test files under `#[cfg(test)]`/`tests/`. The grep `! grep -qiE 'mock|fake|stub' plugins/knowledge/graphiti/plugin.toml scripts/devtools/install-graphiti-mcp.sh scripts/verify/P3-W9-F10.sh` must return empty before first push. The integration-test file's rustdoc may mention 'Mocking ... is forbidden' inside a forbidden-pattern declaration (per WO-0048 line 363 + WO-0069 carve-out).

15. Pinned-immutable-ref policy: confirm `! grep -qE '"(main|latest|master|head|develop|dev|nightly)"' plugins/knowledge/graphiti/plugin.toml` returns empty (the floating-ref class). Commit-SHA pins (40-hex string) and frozen release tags (e.g., `@v0.4.7`) are acceptable; bare branch refs are forbidden. Per master-plan §13 + WO-0069 lesson §executor: floating refs make verifier reruns non-deterministic.

16. The integration-test file's `mod g3_plugin_manifests` block must use `tokio::process::Command` (not `std::process::Command`) for any helper subprocess invocations introduced by this WO — per WO-0075 §executor lesson W1 + WO-0076 pre-emptive carve-out. The existing `mem0_manifest_health_check` does not spawn helper subprocesses (it only calls `PluginManager::health_check_with_timeout` which the daemon-side codepath already routes through `tokio::process::Command`); the new graphiti test SHOULD follow the same minimalist shape with NO net-new helper subprocesses (the load-bearing assertion is the daemon-side spawn + handshake, not an executor-driven side spawn).

17. `unsafe std::env::set_var` IS FORBIDDEN in this WO's test code. If the integration test needs to inject env vars to the spawned MCP server (e.g., a graph-DB-URI for the lifespan handler), pass the env via `Command::env("FALKORDB_URI", ...)` on the spawned child via `PluginManager::spawn`-time API — NOT via parent-process `set_var`. If the daemon-side `PluginManager` does not yet expose a `manifest.transport.env` table (the WO-0076 W1+W2 debt), defer the env-injection-from-test concern entirely: gate the test on the env vars being set at the parent-process level (per scope_in #6 two-gate pattern) and skip-via-env when unset, rather than mutating the parent's env table. NEW unsafe blocks must NOT be introduced by this WO. Per WO-0076 §verifier lesson 'unsafe block without ADR cite — soft critic-warning category'.

18. DEC-0005 module-coherence carve-out applies to the augmentation of tests/g3_plugin_manifests.rs (single small commit adding ONE test inside the existing `mod` block — likely <80 LOC). Cite DEC-0005 + WO-0069 commit `bfd984f` precedent in the relevant commit body. Do NOT split the new test into multiple commits.

19. §15.2 tracing spans do NOT apply to plugin-manifest landings — these features add data files (TOML manifests) + integration tests + helper scripts, NOT new orchestration code. Do NOT introduce new `#[tracing::instrument(...)]` annotations under this WO. Cite WO-0067 lessons §planner #2 ('pure-deterministic / data-only modules: §15.2 tracing does NOT apply') as precedent.

20. WO-0069 lessons §planner #2 carve-out (pre-emptive API-key-gate for plugins requiring keys) APPLIES to this WO: graphiti requires both a graph DB env var AND an LLM API key env var. The verify script's tool-level smoke section MAY short-circuit with `[SKIP] P3-W9-F10: <reason>` + `exit 0` when either gate misses, per scope_in #6 / #11. Critic should NOT flag this as a deviation under this scope_in citation.

21. WO-0069 lessons §planner #3 carve-out (eventual-consistency OR-disjunction): if graphiti's tool-level smoke includes a round-trip assertion against a temporal-validity / fact-graph surface with eventual-consistency semantics (the upstream uses an embedding index for fact retrieval), the assertion MUST be OR-disjunctive (e.g., `if 'wo-0079 episode' not in search_text and 'wo-0079 episode' not in retrieve_text:`) rather than AND-conjunctive. Critic should not flag this as a deviation under this scope_in citation. Per WO-0069 lesson §planner #3 + WO-0070 carry.

22. AC25 reworded per WO-0070 lessons §planner #4: 'feat branch carries zero merge commits' (using `git log feat/WO-0079-graphiti-knowledge-plugin-manifest ^main --merges` returning empty), NOT the 'feat..main = 0' form. Same spirit, more precise wording — tolerates 1-commit divergence between feat and main caused by gate-side critic-report commits between executor RFR push and verifier startup.

23. Carry-forward standing protocol (34 WOs deep, WO-0058..WO-0078): coverage-gate.sh sccache RUSTC_WRAPPER workaround — `env -u RUSTC_WRAPPER cargo llvm-cov --package ucil-daemon --tests --summary-only --json | jq '.data[0].totals.lines.percent'` is the authoritative measurement; the gate-script `coverage-gate.sh ucil-daemon 85 75` reporting `line=0%` is the known sccache interaction. Skip the `[FAIL] coverage gate: ucil-{core,daemon,embeddings}` lines in phase-3.sh output per scope_out + AC23 standing protocol.

24. Carry-forward standing protocol: AC30/AC31 phase-1/phase-2 effectiveness-gate flake (3 open escalations: 20260507T0357Z-effectiveness-nav-rust-symbol-rs-line-flake.md, 20260507T1629Z-effectiveness-refactor-rename-python-fixture-missing-symbol.md, 20260507T1930Z-effectiveness-nav-rust-symbol-doctest-caller-flake.md). Executor runs constituent cargo-test + clippy + MCP-smoke + Serena-docker + diagnostics-bridge sub-checks in isolation; verifier re-runs the gate-script entry point from a clean session. Pre-existing standing scope_out item; awaiting dedicated harness-improvement WO.
25. Verifier MUST NOT set `UCIL_SKIP_EXTERNAL_PLUGIN_TESTS` OR `UCIL_SKIP_KNOWLEDGE_PLUGIN_E2E` envs (per WO-0069 scope_in #5 precedent extended to this WO).

26. RFR (ready-for-review) MUST include the M1/M2 mutation contract section with: (a) named file path, (b) the exact patch (e.g., `s/command = "uvx"/command = "/__ucil_test_nonexistent_M1__"/`), (c) the targeted cargo-test selector, (d) the expected panic line, (e) the restore command (`git checkout -- <path>`), (f) the pre-mutation md5 snapshot path (`/tmp/wo-0079-<file>-orig.md5`). Mirror the WO-0067/0068/0069/0070/0072/0077 RFR Mutation contract section verbatim in shape.

27. Pre-flight `which uvx` before first push — the integration tests will spawn from PATH, and missing `uvx` produces false-positive `PluginError::Spawn { kind: NotFound }` which masks real M1 detection. Per WO-0069 lessons §verifier #2.

28. Pre-baked md5 snapshots: take `md5sum plugins/knowledge/graphiti/plugin.toml > /tmp/wo-0079-graphiti-orig.md5` AND `md5sum crates/ucil-daemon/tests/g3_plugin_manifests.rs > /tmp/wo-0079-g3tests-orig.md5` BEFORE applying any mutation. Confirm restoration via `md5sum -c /tmp/wo-0079-<name>-orig.md5` returning OK after each `git checkout --` restore.

29. WO-0072 (codegraphcontext G4 solo-feature WO) is the canonical reference for the SOLO-FEATURE plugin-manifest WO shape extended with operator-state-CLI-dependent gates per WO-0076. Same shape applied here: ONE feature, ONE manifest, ONE install script, ONE replacement verify script, ONE additive test in an existing peer test file (g3_plugin_manifests.rs), ONE M1 + ONE M2 mutation contract. Avoid 2-feature WOs that would mix groups (G3 + something else).

30. F10 has `attempts=2` (carried from WO-0071-attempt-1 + WO-0071-attempt-2 BLOCKED retries before DEC-0019 deferral, now superseded by DEC-0022). This WO is the **third+final attempt** before the 3-strikes soft escalation triggers (NOT a hard halt; escalation lets RCA inspect). Clean execution required. The blocking factor in WO-0071 was 'no canonical PyPI/npm package' — that's now resolved by the `uvx --from git+...` install path. NO architectural ambiguities remain.

31. DEC-0019's 'carry-forward sentinel' in F10's `blocked_reason` field (currently `null` per the verifier's last_verified_commit `12a705d2`) is obsolete; the verifier will clear it on PASS. No additional cleanup required by this WO.

32. Standing carry: Phase 4 sentinel grep (any future phase-4 WOs that consume graphiti's `knowledge.temporal_query` capability against the live G3 executor) — DEFERRED out-of-scope. F10's `attempts=2 → 3` advance + clean PASS unblocks all such downstream work.
scope_out: 1. Do NOT implement a `G3Source` adapter for graphiti (deferred to a follow-up WO that wires the daemon-side plugin runtime against the F07 G3 executor, which already lives at crates/ucil-daemon/src/g3.rs and ships a `G3Source` trait per WO-0070).
2. Do NOT modify `crates/ucil-daemon/tests/plugin_manifests.rs` (WO-0044 P2-W6-F05/F06 regression guard) — keep that suite isolated per WO-0069 lessons §executor #2.
3. Do NOT modify the existing two tests inside `mod g3_plugin_manifests { ... }` (codebase_memory_manifest_health_check, mem0_manifest_health_check) — only ADD the third graphiti test.
4. Do NOT add new G3 plugin manifests beyond Graphiti under this WO (arc-memory, cognee, conport are all future-WO scope per master-plan §14.1).

5. Do NOT wire the new manifest into any `PluginManager::initialize_all` / startup-discovery path beyond what already exists — the WO-0044 + WO-0069 manifests are loaded on-demand by the integration tests via `PluginManifest::from_path(&manifest_path)`, and the new manifest should be loadable the same way without daemon-startup changes.

6. Do NOT introduce or modify any LLM-provider or embedding-provider configuration (graphiti's optional LLM backend is operator-scoped; UCIL only consumes the MCP `tools/list` surface, not the underlying LLM).

7. Do NOT introduce graph-DB sidecar provisioning (FalkorDB / Neo4j docker-compose orchestration) — that is operator-state per WO-0077 §planner-classification: graphiti is operator-state-DUAL-DEPENDENT (graph DB + LLM API key), gated via env-var presence checks.
8. Do NOT introduce any cross-WO dependency wiring (downstream WOs consume F10; that's their scope).
9. Do NOT add any new `tracing::instrument` annotations to source code (per scope_in #19).
10. Do NOT add or modify entries in `ucil-build/feature-list.json` — verifier flips `passes` via `scripts/flip-feature.sh`.
11. Do NOT modify any file under `tests/fixtures/**` — verify scripts are read-only against the fixture (per WO-0069 forbidden_paths).
12. Do NOT alter `scripts/gate/**` or `scripts/flip-feature.sh` (forbidden_paths).

13. Do NOT modify the SECONDARY install path (docker-image) infrastructure unless PRIMARY (uvx --from git+) is verified to fail. Per scope_in #2 — UCIL has zero docker-installed plugins; do not introduce one as a side-effect of this WO.

14. Standing carry: do NOT touch `scripts/verify/coverage-gate.sh` to fix the sccache `RUSTC_WRAPPER` interaction — that is a Bucket B / Bucket D harness-improvement candidate per WO-0058..WO-0078 lessons. Per scope_in #23 standing protocol.
15. Standing carry: do NOT remediate the three pre-existing effectiveness-gate flake escalations under this WO — those await a dedicated harness-improvement WO. Per scope_in #24 standing protocol.
16. Do NOT add `#[ignore]`, `.skip()`, or commented-out assertions to silence any test (anti-laziness contract).
17. Do NOT use `todo!()`, `unimplemented!()`, or `pass`-only bodies (anti-laziness contract).

18. Standing carry: do NOT attempt to fix `scripts/reality-check.sh` pre-existing-stash bug (carries from WO-0067/0068/0069/0070/0072/0073/0074/0075/0076/0077; informational-only at this point — the M1/M2 in-place mutation contract is the authoritative anti-laziness layer).

19. Do NOT introduce `unsafe std::env::set_var` in test code (per scope_in #17 + WO-0076 W2 carry-forward debt). The graph-DB-URI / LLM-API-key env-var injection (if needed) is gated at the parent-process level via the verify script's two-gate short-circuit, NOT mutated by the test code.

20. Do NOT supersede DEC-0022 in this WO's commits — that ADR is the authoritative reversal of DEC-0019 and lives in `ucil-build/decisions/`. The forbidden_paths list excludes `ucil-build/decisions/**` from executor edits.

21. Do NOT widen the integration test's expected-tool-name pin to a count-based assertion (`tools.len() >= N`). Per WO-0069 lessons §planner: pinning on a specific tool name surfaces upstream tool-surface drift loudly; pinning on a count tolerates benign upstream tool additions but masks tool-rename / tool-removal regressions.
acceptance: 1. plugins/knowledge/graphiti/plugin.toml exists, is valid TOML, and parses cleanly via `PluginManifest::from_path` to {name='graphiti', version=<pinned commit SHA short form>, transport.kind='stdio', capabilities.provides non-empty, capabilities.provides[0] starts with 'knowledge.'}.

2. plugins/knowledge/graphiti/plugin.toml's `[transport]` section uses `command = "uvx"` (bare binary name, NOT absolute path) and `args` containing `--from`, `git+https://github.com/getzep/graphiti.git@<COMMIT-SHA>#subdirectory=mcp_server`, `<entry-point-name>` per scope_in #3.

3. The graphiti manifest's leading comment block establishes the operator-state-DUAL-DEPENDENT classification (graph DB env var + LLM API key env var) and documents the secondary docker-image install path per scope_in #2.
4. scripts/devtools/install-graphiti-mcp.sh exists, is executable, exits 0, and prints OK / MISSING branches correctly per the operator's environment.

5. crates/ucil-daemon/tests/g3_plugin_manifests.rs's `mod g3_plugin_manifests` block contains THREE tests (codebase_memory_manifest_health_check, mem0_manifest_health_check, graphiti_manifest_health_check); the existing two tests are byte-identical to pre-WO state.
6. `cargo test -p ucil-daemon --test g3_plugin_manifests g3_plugin_manifests::graphiti_manifest_health_check` exits 0 with `1 passed; 0 failed` summary.

7. scripts/verify/P3-W9-F10.sh (replacement, no longer a TODO-stub) exits 0 with the cargo-test summary line confirmed and the tool-level smoke section either firing OK or `[SKIP]`-ing per the two-gate short-circuit per scope_in #6 / #11.

8. M1 mutation (graphiti transport.command poison) flips the `g3_plugin_manifests::graphiti_manifest_health_check` test from PASS to FAIL with a spawn-error chain (`PluginError::Spawn { source: Os { code: 2, kind: NotFound } }`); restoration via `git checkout -- plugins/knowledge/graphiti/plugin.toml` + md5 match returns the test to PASS.

9.
